name: find_files
description: Find files by extension/type under a given path, with optional path inclusion/exclusion filters, sorting, and result limits. Supports Python, Go, shell, config, and test files in one call.
when_to_use: Use at the beginning of a task or anytime you need to locate files of specific types in a project. Replaces multiple 'find' and 'ls' calls with different filters. Preferred over ad-hoc find commands for routine file discovery.

parameters.name: extensions
parameters.description: Comma-separated file extensions to find, e.g. 'py,go,sh' (default: 'py')

parameters.name: include_path
parameters.description: Only include paths matching this pattern (e.g. 'pkg/services')

parameters.name: max_results
parameters.description: Maximum number of results to show (default: 50, use 0 for unlimited)

examples.expected: List of up to 50 Python files under /app, excluding venv and __pycache__.
Sorted list of Go and shell files under /app/pkg/services.
Sorted list of up to 200 Python files, excluding migrations and venv directories.
Go files under /app related to ngalert.

cost_saving_rationale: Combines 2-3 separate find commands (with different extensions, filters, sorting) into a single script call, reducing round trips. The exclusion patterns and result limits prevent token-wasting output of irrelevant files.
